title: V refinement of video motion vectors in adjacent video data
abstract: A method of decoding video is provided, comprising: determining that refined motion vectors of a spatially neighboring set to be available when the refined motion vectors have been computed, wherein the availability of the refined motion vectors of the spatially neighboring set comprising at least one coding unit or sub-coding unit for a current set comprising at least one coding unit or sub coding unit; and partitioning a coding unit normatively into as many sub-coding units as the number of concurrency sets that the coding unit spans so that the data pre-fetch and decoder-side motion vector refinement and motion compensation for each sub-coding unit can happen independent of the other sub coding-units, but in a concurrent manner with other coding or sub-coding units that are part of a current concurrency set.
claims: Claims ( 17 ) AĂ‚Â methodĂ‚Â ofĂ‚Â decodingĂ‚Â forĂ‚Â videoĂ‚Â data,Ă‚Â suchĂ‚Â asĂ‚Â inĂ‚Â DMRVĂ‚Â orĂ‚Â PMMVD,Ă‚Â implementedĂ‚Â byĂ‚Â aĂ‚Â decodingĂ‚Â unitĂ‚Â orĂ‚Â anĂ‚Â encodingĂ‚Â unit,Ă‚Â comprising: -Ă‚Â determining,Ă‚Â forĂ‚Â aĂ‚Â currentĂ‚Â setĂ‚Â whichĂ‚Â comprisesĂ‚Â atĂ‚Â leastĂ‚Â oneĂ‚Â codingĂ‚Â unitĂ‚Â (CU)Ă‚Â ,Ă‚Â orĂ‚Â subĂ‚Â codingĂ‚Â unit,Ă‚Â thatĂ‚Â aĂ‚Â refinedĂ‚Â motionĂ‚Â vectorĂ‚Â ofĂ‚Â aĂ‚Â spatiallyĂ‚Â neighboringĂ‚Â setĂ‚Â isĂ‚Â availableĂ‚Â whenĂ‚Â theĂ‚Â refinedĂ‚Â motionĂ‚Â vectorsĂ‚Â haveĂ‚Â beenĂ‚Â obtained,Ă‚Â whereinĂ‚Â theĂ‚Â spatiallyĂ‚Â neighboringĂ‚Â setĂ‚Â comprisesĂ‚Â atĂ‚Â leastĂ‚Â oneĂ‚Â codingĂ‚Â unitĂ‚Â (CU)Ă‚Â orĂ‚Â sub-codingĂ‚Â unit;Ă‚Â andĂ‚Â -providingĂ‚Â theĂ‚Â saidĂ‚Â refinedĂ‚Â motionĂ‚Â vectorĂ‚Â toĂ‚Â aĂ‚Â currentĂ‚Â processingĂ‚Â stageĂ‚Â ofĂ‚Â aĂ‚Â processingĂ‚Â pipelineĂ‚Â forĂ‚Â theĂ‚Â currentĂ‚Â set.
